description: The Excel sjövatten sheet uses a virtual residual meter "BPS_V2" (= B342.V2_VM90_V + B342.V2_VM91_V − 15 direct consumer meters) that is fractionally distributed to FIVE buildings with R-factors summing to 1.0: B301 @ 0.09, B302 @ 0.18, B303 @ 0.18, B307 @ 0.46, B344 @ 0.09. Coefficients vary month-to-month in the live sheet's hidden R-column; a static-coefficient approximation would be acceptable if the drain side were complete. 2026-04-21 investigation: attempted to materialise a BPS_V2 virtual-meter pool (1 SNV-campus virtual + 5 building share virtuals, 15 hasSubMeter drains, 2 inlet refs summed via a derived sum-ref, 5 outgoing feeds at the R-factors). Mechanics worked (sum over sum-ref inlet produces single-row-per-timestamp flow; multi-feed double-count bug avoided by aggregating inlets at the timeseries layer). BUT: 8 of the 15 direct-consumer meters have no Snowflake data (STRUX-only: B304-52-V2-AW026, B310.V2_GF4_1, B330.V2_GF4_1, B337.V2_GF4_1, TE-52-V2-GF4:1 Kringlan, TE-52-V2-SCANIA; offline pre-2026-01: B305.V2_VMM21, B339.V2_GF4), so the ontology-computed residual is ~34× larger than Excel's (~204k vs 6k m³ in Jan 2026). Building shares came out ~34× too large, which is worse than the pre-existing zero-attribution. Reverted. Re-attempt becomes viable once the 8 STRUX-only/offline meters have Snowflake counter data. Jan-2026 residuals (ontology vs Excel): B301 −540, B302 −1081, B303 −1081, B307 −2761, B344 −540 kWh. Classify as excel_cooked_coefficient.

B304-52-V2-AW026 is a manually-read STRUX meter with no Snowflake equivalent. Excel caches the meter's monthly value; ontology reads zero. B304 therefore under-counts by the STRUX cached value each month (−29640 Jan-2026, −43483 Feb-2026). Per project convention, STRUX-only meters are never synthesized as Snowflake rows.

Counter went offline 2025-08-05 at value 335549.97. Replaced with a new device that came online 2025-11-30 at pre-loaded baseline value 42668.71 (factory / commission offset, not consumption). Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum; the classifier treats the boundary as a device swap (anchor to new raw, cumulative held flat across the boundary) rather than a reset, so the 42,668 baseline is not mistakenly recorded as one-day consumption. The ~4-month gap (Aug 5 → Nov 29) is flat-carried; physical flow during that period is unrecovered. Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference period so no facit impact.
Device swap 2025-02-17 (last value 758607.50). Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference period.
Counter went offline 2026-02-20 at value 9471341.0. Inside Feb 2026 reference period — affects last 8 days of the month.
Counter went offline 2025-10-18 at value 6.60 (essentially zero before offline too). Outside Jan/Feb 2026.
Counter went offline 2025-05-19 at value 4840979.0. Outside Jan/Feb 2026.

Seawater cooling seasonal pattern. Active Jun-Oct (~40-90 L/day summer peak), minimal Nov-May (mostly flat days with occasional 1-2 L/day). Not a data-quality issue — reflects physical use of seawater cooling during warm months.

Inverse seasonal pattern. Active Dec-May (~50-115k L/month), zero Jun-Nov (6-month dormancy). Likely a winter-use circuit (possibly heat-recovery or free-cooling dependent on seawater temperature). Not a data-quality issue. The 'spike' visible on 2026-02-28 is simply the normal daily rate during its active season.

B301 = 0.09 × BPS_V2 in Excel (sheet-level fractional residual pool). See ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool for the pool mechanics; revisit is blocked pending fractional-subtract primitive and complete Snowflake coverage of the BPS drain meters.
B302 = 0.18 × BPS_V2 share. Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.
B303 = 0.18 × BPS_V2 share. Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.
B307 = 0.46 × BPS_V2 share (largest share). Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.
B344 = 0.09 × BPS_V2 share. Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.

BKringlan is an external-consumer (Telge Nät) metered via TE-52-V2-GF4:1, a manually-read STRUX meter with no BMS data. Excel shows cached STRUX values; ontology reports zero. Per project convention, STRUX-only meters are not synthesized into Snowflake.
BScania is an external-consumer (Telge Nät / Scania) metered via TE-52-V2-SCANIA, a manually-read STRUX meter with no BMS data. Same convention as BKringlan.

Reference formula doc sheet 'SJÖVATTEN V2' exists but has no populated formulas (header only). Current workstream ontology predates the doc and continues to be authoritative. Re-check when the doc is populated.
